summary.intent: Implement a ScrollArea agnostic core component (task #279) for the ars-ui Rust UI library, following existing component patterns for state machines, effects, and adapter boundaries.
summary.outcome: A complete ScrollArea state machine was implemented with 5 Codex review passes addressing 21 findings, achieving 62 unit tests, 13 snapshot fixtures, proptest and spec-conformance coverage, and 97.48% line / 98.39% region coverage. PR #699 is pending final Codex approval after the session limit was hit mid-pass-5.

repo: Timer/timeout effects follow a named marker pattern (e.g., Effect::AutoHide / PendingEffect::named) where the adapter owns the actual timer, not the core — matching tooltip and rating_group conventions.
hide_delay uses plain Duration (not u32 ms, not Option<Duration>) with a default value, consistent with tooltip/hover_card/avatar components.
RTL scroll normalization should use the canonical ars_collections::normalize_scroll_left_rtl helper with RtlScrollMode rather than inline logic.
Local Axis enum uses {X, Y} variants while ars-dom uses {Horizontal, Vertical} — a cross-cutting rename has been deferred as known debt.
Pre-existing clippy errors in sibling modules (e.g., pagination map_or_else) can block all clippy runs on the entire crate and must be fixed as a side-effect.
context_only closures require explicit &mut Context type annotations due to Rust inference limitations.
gh CLI API reply calls require -F (integer flag) not -f (string flag) for in_reply_to parameters.
context_changed is set whenever an apply closure runs (not value-diff), so no-op transitions still report changed.
set_state_for_test is #[cfg(test)] in ars-core — unavailable cross-crate, so unreachable defensive guards in the core cannot be tested.

code.finding: Orientation gating: Context.orientation + ScrollOrientation::allows_x/allows_y helpers required to gate visibility flags per axis in part_attrs.
on_props_changed was missing; prop changes (scrollbar_visibility, hide_delay, etc.) never synced into Context until remount without Event::SyncProps.
AutoHide setup closure body is adapter-invoked by design; the core emits a named PendingEffect::named marker and the adapter owns timer lifecycle.
Resize handler did not clear stale visibility flags in Hover/Scroll modes — update_visibility(active: bool) param required for state-aware clears.
MouseLeaveScrollbar could leave state stuck; leave events must be guarded to no-op when ThumbDragging is active.
ThumbDragMove lacked an upper clamp; new_thumb_pos must be clamped to [0, track_size - thumb_size] to prevent scroll offset beyond content bounds.
ThumbDragEnd did not restart the AutoHide timer in Scroll mode, leaving scrollbar visible forever after a drag.
compute_thumb_metrics else 0.0 branch is unreachable after the early content<=viewport return — justified uncovered line.
Contains the canonical normalize_scroll_left_rtl(raw, content, viewport, mode: RtlScrollMode) helper; scroll_area's inline sign-heuristic was wrong at raw==0 for positive mode.
Pre-existing map_or_else(identity) pattern denied by Rust stable 1.96.0's new clippy lint — blocks all clippy runs on ars-components; requires a one-line fix.

workflow: A 3-phase post-implementation audit (spec/impl drift → adjacent surfaces → coverage) catches systemic issues before Codex review, reducing review round count.
cargo xci-fast (10 gates) must be green after every fix batch before committing — running it per-patch prevents compounding gate failures.
Codex review findings should be triaged before implementing: 'clear bug' vs 'judgment call requiring user input' avoids over-fixing on contested design decisions.
Named test functions per finding (e.g., test_thumb_drag_clamp_upper_bound) make it easy to reply to review threads with specific test names as proof.
Background snapshot jobs can race; always wait for them and run a clean single pass rather than relying on parallel snap accept.

friction: context_only closures required explicit &mut Context type annotations throughout, causing multiple rounds of compiler errors after initial write.
Background snapshot jobs raced with each other, requiring manual cleanup of stale .snap.pending files.
insta --accept was blocked by pre-existing i18n-gated snapshot fixtures, requiring manual promotion of only the scroll_area snapshots.
drop(api) pattern triggered clippy warnings; required block-scope refactoring to eliminate.
Pre-existing pagination map_or_else clippy error blocked all clippy runs on ars-components and had to be fixed as a side-effect.
gh CLI used -f instead of -F for in_reply_to, stringifying integers and causing API failures on the first reply attempt.
21 findings across 5 Codex review passes — each pass surfaced edge cases introduced by the previous round's fixes.
Session limit hit mid-pass-5, leaving final Codex reply/resolve/re-trigger incomplete.
open_items: Pass 5 Codex threads not yet replied-to, resolved, or re-triggered — session ended before completion.
PR #699 merge to main and issue #279 close are pending Codex 👍 approval.
Adapter implementations (Leptos and Dioxus ScrollArea components) are out of scope for this PR and need separate tasks.
Axis{X,Y} vs ars-dom Axis{Horizontal,Vertical} naming inconsistency is a known cross-cutting rename, explicitly deferred.
Coverage measurement (97.48% line) was last taken after pass 3; final number after passes 4 and 5 additions is unverified.
